_doc: Vendor-example strings that appear inside shipped library/SDK code and served bundles, present in nearly every app that uses that vendor. A grep hit on one of these proves NOTHING about the app's own configuration. Any claim of the form 'artifact X carries wrong value V' where V is listed here is a phantom until the paired GOOD-value read is produced (bipolar search). Seed and extend as new phantoms are identified.

phantoms.why: Example deployment subdomain baked into Convex SDK error messages and docs; ships inside every Convex app bundle. A grep match is vendored library text, not the app's CONVEX_URL.
phantoms.good_value_check: vercel env pull / GET project env, OR grep .env.local for the app's real NEXT_PUBLIC_CONVEX_URL — read the value RETURNED, never a bundle grep.

phantoms.why: The Convex example-URL shape (adjective-animal-number). If it appears in a served bundle it is almost always the SDK example, not the app deployment. Match IN CONTEXT (surrounding line) before calling it configuration.
phantoms.good_value_check: Read the deployment identity the app RETURNS (env pull / convex status), never infer from a bundle string.
